title: mfa — field classification
description: MFA rows carry two classes of sensitive data: (1) cryptographic material (WebAuthn public keys, credential IDs, TOTP secrets — the latter live on identity/identity's Identity row) — `regulated_secret`; (2) device-recognition metadata (transports, AAGUID, sign_count) — `confidential`. Recovery-code plaintext is EMITTED-ONCE at enrolment / regeneration and thereafter exists only as bcrypt hashes on the Identity row (owned by identity/identity). Every field on the WebauthnCredential row is REDACTED by default at the audit layer.

public: WebauthnCredential.device_name (user-provided nickname — e.g. 'iPhone Face ID'; treated as public because the user chooses it and it appears in the picker UI at login)
confidential: WebauthnCredential.sign_count (monotonic replay-protection counter — leaking it doesn't compromise the key but does expose usage frequency)
MfaChallenge.attempts_remaining (leaks how far a brute-forcer got — resolved from the shared auth_mfa_challenges table by identity/auth)
regulated_secret: WebauthnCredential.credential_id (bytea — the credential handle emitted by the authenticator; combined with public_key it authenticates the caller. NEVER logged, NEVER emitted on metrics labels, NEVER returned unmuted on wire — the base64url accessor `credential_id_b64url` IS emitted because the browser needs it to construct the assertion request; the raw bytea stays server-side)
WebauthnCredential.public_key (bytea, COSE-encoded — the credential's public key)

MfaEnrollmentPreview.totp_secret (base32 — held in server memory only during the two-round enrolment; NEVER persisted before round 2 confirmation; discarded from memory the moment the preview response is dispatched)
RecoveryCodesDownload.codes (plaintext array — emitted ONCE at enrolment / regeneration; caller must persist client-side or the codes are lost)

wire_projection.description: WebauthnCredential is returned to the tenant caller on the /me/mfa/webauthn/credentials list + individual read endpoints. Every field marked `regulated_secret` is dropped at the wire boundary; every field marked `internal` is included but stays server-side by default in logs / metrics. The MfaEnrollmentPreview + RecoveryCodesDownload DTOs are the ONLY surfaces that emit plaintext secrets — both are single-shot endpoints whose responses are never cached and whose plaintext is deliberately never persisted.

tenant_wire_shape.credential_id_b64url: base64url-encoded credential_id (needed by the browser at login to construct the assertion request)
tenant_wire_shape.transports: WebauthnCredential.transports (array of usb / nfc / ble / internal / hybrid)

never_returned: WebauthnCredential.credential_id (raw bytea — regulated_secret; only the base64url accessor is emitted, and only for the login-side assertion construction)
WebauthnCredential.aaguid (internal — occasionally emitted on platform-admin surfaces for authenticator-model auditing)

shape.identity_id: resolved from the authenticated session — never accepted from user input

input_dtos.compliance_gate: Refused when the caller's tenant does not hold the `mfa_totp` entitlement OR the `mfa.enrollment_enabled` feature flag is off.

shape.totp_secret: string (base32 — echoed back from the preview response; validated against the ephemeral secret held server-side)
shape.totp_confirmation_code: string (6-digit numeric — proves the user scanned the secret into their authenticator app)

shape.authenticator_selection: string ('platform' | 'cross-platform' | 'any') — hint to browsers about which authenticator type to prompt for

shape.attestation_response: object (raw WebAuthn attestation response from navigator.credentials.create)
shape.device_name: string (user-provided nickname, max 60 chars, validated by valid_device_name rule)

shape.challenge_id: ULID (`mfc_...`) — resolved from the challenge ledger
shape.method: enum: totp | webauthn | recovery_code — must match the method the challenge was issued for
shape.webauthn_assertion: object | null (raw WebAuthn assertion response when method=webauthn)
shape.recovery_code: string | null (10 chars from Crockford base32 alphabet when method=recovery_code, validated by valid_recovery_code_format rule)

shape.user_id: string (ULID) — the User whose Identity's MFA should be force-disabled

output_dtos.purpose: Response for GET /api/v1/me/mfa. Summarises which methods the caller has enrolled + the recovery-codes remaining.

shape.step_up_required_for: array of action IDs the caller has entitlement to but where the recent-challenge freshness has expired

output_dtos.purpose: Response for POST /api/v1/me/mfa/totp/enroll/preview. Exposes plaintext secret ONCE — never persisted before round 2.

shape.totp_secret: string (base32 — held server-side in a signed short-lived envelope; caller re-submits on confirm)
shape.totp_uri: otpauth:// URI for QR-code rendering by the SPA
shape.account_label: string (email fingerprint, NOT plaintext email — keeps regulated_minor identities safe in QR-code scans)
shape.expires_at: timestamp (preview is valid for 5 minutes; the caller must confirm within that window OR run PurgeUnconfirmedEnrollmentsJob wipes the ephemeral state)

output_dtos.purpose: Response for POST /api/v1/me/mfa/recovery-codes/regenerate + POST /me/mfa/recovery-codes/download. Emits plaintext codes ONCE.

shape.codes: string[] (10 codes, 10 chars each from Crockford base32 alphabet)
shape.expires_at: null (codes never expire; regeneration is the only invalidation path)

output_dtos.purpose: Response for POST /api/v1/me/mfa/webauthn/register/options. The browser passes this to navigator.credentials.create().

shape.challenge_b64url: string (base64url-encoded 32-byte random challenge, retained server-side in the challenge ledger)
shape.user_display_name: string (User's Profile display_name, resolved from the referring User)
shape.excluded_credentials: array (already-registered credential IDs — prevents duplicate registration of the same physical key)

output_dtos.purpose: Response for GET /api/v1/me/mfa/webauthn/credentials + PATCH updates. Standard wire shape — see wire_projection above.

notes: The TOTP plaintext secret NEVER touches durable storage before the round-2 confirmation. During round 1 it lives inside a signed envelope held in Redis (short TTL, 5 min) under a key derived from the caller's session + a fresh nonce; PurgeUnconfirmedEnrollmentsJob evicts stale entries.

Recovery-code plaintext is emitted ONCE per generation event and NEVER retrievable again. The client (or the user) must persist them — an /me/mfa/recovery-codes/download endpoint exists for the current codes but requires a fresh MFA challenge and is audit-logged as a data-egress event.

The `credential_id_b64url` accessor IS emitted to the client on the WebAuthn login flow — the browser needs it to disambiguate which registered credential to use in navigator.credentials.get(). The raw bytea column is `regulated_secret` but the base64url projection is confidential-tier (needed for interoperability with the W3C JavaScript API).

AAGUID is technically PII-adjacent (identifies the authenticator model — e.g. YubiKey 5C NFC vs iCloud Keychain vs Windows Hello), but not the individual key. We classify it internal + emit on platform-admin surfaces only, so tenant callers don't see which model each other's Users hold. This supports a stronger corporate-security posture where hardware-key inventory is admin-only knowledge.
